text:
Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. The nurse had to _____ the correct dosage of antibiotics before the patient's fever worsened. a. grab b. arise c. administer d. regain
2. Mr Ashraf asked Leen to pay close _____ while he was demonstrating the main points. a. attention b. consciousness c. calmness d. compression
3. The movie about the war orphans was truly _____. a. heartbreaking b. rewarding c. exhausted d. typical
4. Saving a patient's life can be one of the most _____ experiences for a doctor or nurse. a. heartbreaking b. rewarding c. exhausted d. typical
5. During the night _____, the nurse checked on each patient twice. a. chaos b. shift c. care d. procedure
6. The team are now ready for the sudden _____. a. emergency b. shift c. care d. procedure
7. Rodayna described her experience as an emotional _____ of highs and lows. a. chaos b. patience c. CPR d. rollercoaster
8. He is a _____ of his bad health habits. a. view b. value c. victim d. villain
9. The emergency room was full of noise, movement, and complete _____. a. chaos b. patience c. challenge d. progress
10. When the alarm rang, Mariam had to _____ her bag quickly and rush to the emergency room. a. grab b. administer c. arise d. regain

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
11. The paramedic checked the patient's _____ to see if he was awake and aware of his surroundings. a. attention b. consciousness c. calmness d. compression
12. The athlete began to _____ from dehydration and was carried away. a. collapse b. assist c. handle d. care
13. Mr Ayman performed _____ on the unconscious man until help arrived. a. USA b. ARE c. CPR d. HR
14. The _____ helped calm down the patient before reaching the hospital. a. paramedic b. colleague c. victim d. farmer
15. After severe pain in the _____, the man was taken to the hospital. a. challenge b. pulse c. heart attack d. chest
16. The doctor placed two fingers on the patient's wrist to check his _____. a. challenge b. pulse c. heart attack d. chest
17. After several weeks of physical therapy, Uncle Omar began to _____ strength in his injured arm. a. grab b. administer c. arise d. regain
18. During emergencies, showing _____ is essential to make clear and effective decisions. a. pulse b. consciousness c. calmness d. compression
19. Surgeon Alaa explained the _____ step-by-step before the operation began. a. chaos b. shift c. care d. procedure
20. It's important to get _____ ready before handling high-stress situations. a. anxiously b. mentally c. elderly d. friendly

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
21. The nurse handed the correct _____ to the doctor during the procedure. a. poison b. victim c. victim d. medication
22. This hospital provides excellent _____ for elderly patients. a. victim b. emergency c. care d. procedure
23. When the alarm went off, everyone had to _____ to the emergency room. a. ensure b. document c. rush d. review
24. The teacher praised Sama for her _____ during exams. a. emergency b. compression c. chance d. progress
25. The team were in _____ when two alarms sounded together. a. satisfaction b. surgery c. teamwork d. confusion
26. Unexpected complications may _____ during any surgical operation, so doctors must always be prepared. a. grab b. arise c. administer d. regain
27. To stop the bleeding, the paramedic applied direct _____ to the wound using a clean cloth. a. conservation b. consciousness c. conversation d. compression
28. Nurses must treat all patients with kindness and _____. a. chaos b. shift c. care d. procedure
29. This hospital offers special services for _____ patients who may need extra assistance. a. waiting b. anxiously c. elderly d. fulfilled
30. Please, _____ that the patient receives the right dosage of medicine at the right time. a. ensure b. document c. rush d. review

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
31. Treating burn victims is a difficult medical _____. a. emotion b. patience c. challenge d. progress
32. The surgeon recommended an immediate _____ to remove the tumor. a. disorder b. surgery c. teamwork d. confusion
33. The intercom _____ loudly, which alerted the staff to check Room 3 immediately. a. buzzed b. slept c. progressed d. typical
34. After a 12-hour shift at hospital, the nurse felt completely _____. a. complained b. rewarding c. exhausted d. typically
35. We waited _____ for the doctor to return with the test results. a. anxiously b. mentally c. totally d. chest
36. CPR was performed immediately to save the patient from a _____. a. challenge b. pulse c. heart attack d. chest
37. Nurse Aziza felt great _____ seeing the patient recover. a. weakness b. satisfaction c. dependence d. correction
38. Mrs Eman works with her _____ to finish the task in time. a. paramedics b. colleagues c. victims d. enemies
39. Doctors must know how to _____ pressure in life-threatening cases. a. collapse b. assist c. handle d. care
40. Doctor Mustafa _____ the patient's charts before beginning surgery. a. ensured b. documented c. rushed d. reviewed

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
41. Nurse Sara monitored the patient's _____ every hour. a. emergency b. patience c. challenge d. progress
42. Cooperation is a key to successful _____ during emergencies. a. disorder b. surgery c. teamwork d. confusion
43. The machine _____ loudly when the heart rate dropped. a. complained b. grabbed c. beeped d. progressed
44. A/An _____ day in an emergency room usually involves tens of patients and fast decisions. a. heartbreaking b. rewarding c. exhausted d. typical
45. After years of helping students, Mr Mohammed finally felt _____ in his career. a. anxiously b. mentally c. heart attack d. fulfilled
46. Working in the emergency is a daily _____ for many medical staff. a. challenge b. pulse c. elderly d. care
47. Uncle Omar felt sudden _____ in his muscles and had to sit down immediately. a. weakness b. satisfaction c. heart attack d. chest
48. Nurses _____ surgeons by preparing the tools. a. collapse b. assist c. surgery d. challenge
49. Nurses must _____ all treatments in the patient's file. a. ensure b. document c. handle d. care
50. Brain _____ are often caused by a lack of chemical balance. a. disorders b. surgeries c. rush d. assist

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
51. The government announced a sudden policy _____ that confused many departments. a. attention b. shift c. teamwork d. confusion
52. The actor forgot his lines when the director called for his full _____ during rehearsal. a. attention b. administer c. shift d. collapse
53. The stock market experienced a sharp _____ after the global crisis. a. attention b. shift c. administer d. collapse

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. To _____ is to suddenly fall down, often because of illness or exhaustion. a. grab b. collapse c. administer d. beep
2. A/An _____ is the front part of the human body between the neck and the stomach. a. chest b. consciousness c. emergency d. paramedic
3. _____ is a state of total confusion and lack of order. a. Attention b. Chaos c. CPR d. Pulse
4. The act of focusing or carefully listening or watching is known as _____. a. attention b. chaos c. CPR d. pulse
5. A/An _____ is a trained medical professional who provides emergency care outside the hospital. a. oculist b. engineer c. psychologist d. paramedic
6. To _____ is to give medicine or treatment to someone. a. grab b. collapse c. administer d. beep
7. _____ is the state of being awake and aware of your surroundings. a. Chest b. Consciousness c. Emergency d. Paramedic
8. A life-saving technique involving chest compressions and rescue breathing is called _____. a. attention b. chaos c. CPR d. pulse
9. To _____ is to take or hold something quickly, especially with your hands. a. grab b. collapse c. administer d. beep
10. _____ means causing deep emotional pain or sadness. a. Rewarding b. Heartbreaking c. Cardiopulmonary d. Emergent
11. _____ is a serious and sudden situation requiring immediate attention. a. Kick off b. Crime c. Emergency d. Support
12. To _____ is to make a short high sound, often from a machine or device. a. grab b. collapse c. administer d. beep
13. _____ is a regular beat of the heart felt through the skin. a. Attention b. Dance c. Glance d. Pulse

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. Please, pay due _____ to the safety procedures before you begin. a. attentively b. attention c. attentive d. inattentive
2. The staff remained calm during the _____. a. emergence b. emergently c. emergency d. emergent
3. My grandma lost _____ after falling and hitting her head. a. conscious b. unconscious c. consciously d. consciousness
4. That opening sentence was a real attention _____. a. grab b. grabbed c. grabber d. grabs
5. Mr Rashedy wanted to _____ students' attention via brainstorming. a. grab b. grabbing c. grabber d. grabbed
6. The athlete received a _____ for helping the injured opponent. a. rewarding b. reward c. rewarded d. rewards

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. A nurse has to _____ medication to the patients carefully. a. make b. reflect c. administer d. document
2. Joury _____ her day with a healthy breakfast. a. kicks off b. regains c. checks d. does
3. After the accident, I had to _____ CPR to an injured child. a. make b. administer c. do d. reflect
4. Athletes should always _____ their pulse after violent exercise. a. make b. check c. take d. document
5. Patience, kindness and skills help _____ a great nurse over time. a. reflect b. do c. make d. kick
6. At the end of the day, Rodayna likes to _____ on what she has gone through during the day. a. reflect b. regain c. start d. check

Choose the TWO correct answers out of the FIVE options given:
1. "Sama paid close attention to what I said." The word 'attention' here is a synonym of _____. a. ignorance b. focus c. silence d. mess e. concentration
2. "The emergency room is no place for chaos." The word 'chaos' here is an antonym of _____. a. harmony b. panic c. order d. storm e. confusion
3. "Omar collapsed on the floor after running." The word 'collapsed' here is an antonym of _____. a. fainted b. stumbled c. recovered d. rose e. fell
4. "The patient finally regained consciousness." The word 'consciousness' here is a synonym of _____. a. sleep b. awareness c. alertness d. dizziness e. blackout
5. "They acted fast in the emergency." The word 'emergency' here is an antonym of _____. a. normalcy b. disaster c. routine d. urgency e. danger
6. "Ahmed quickly grabbed the falling glass." The word 'grabbed' here is a synonym of _____. a. threw b. seized c. clutched d. ignored e. let go

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. After helping all her patients, Mariam felt exhausted but ________. a. upset b. fulfilled c. dizzy d. sleepy
2. When the accident happened, the hospital staff had to ________ immediately. a. wait b. spring into action c. fall back d. relax
3. Mariam always says, "________" as she values cooperation. a. Patience is power b. Be calm and carry on c. Teamwork makes the dream work d. Practice makes perfect
4. The emergency room buzzes ________ activity during the midday shift. a. at b. on c. with d. in
5. The nurse cared ________ the injured man until the doctor arrived. a. to b. under c. for d. across
6. Several patients complained ________ severe headaches after taking the medication. a. in b. of c. at d. over
7. He slipped on the wet floor and fell ________ instantly. a. out b. down c. above d. through
8. After losing the final match, the team decided to move ________ and focus on the future events. a. in b. up c. on d. in
9. The meeting usually kicks ________ at exactly 8 p.m. a. in b. off c. down d. over
10. Before making a decision, I always reflect ________ past experience. a. on b. at c. at d. from
11. In difficult times, true friends are the ones you can rely ________ for support. a. from b. at c. on d. up

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. We need to ________ more farm animals for meat and milk. a. raise b. rise c. arise d. arouse
2. If any problem ________ call me immediately. a. raises b. arises c. arouses d. rises
3. Her speech has ________ great interest among the attendants. a. risen b. failed c. aroused d. raised
4. After finishing the project, let's move ________ to the next task. a. in b. on c. into d. onto
5. Mr Ayman plans to move ________ a new apartment next week near his workplace. a. on b. onto c. into d. up
6. It took years of hard work before Mrs Noha finally felt ________ in her career. a. full b. filled c. fulfilled d. failed
7. The bottle was completely ________ with cold water. a. full b. filled c. filled d. fulfilled
8. Cairo Stadium was ________ of fans by the time the match started. a. filled b. failed c. full d. fulfilled

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. I ________ bought some flour. a. have b. has c. have been d. has been
2. Some flour ________ bought. a. have b. has c. have been d. has been
3. She ________ fed the babies. a. have b. has c. have been d. has been
4. The babies ________ fed. a. have b. has c. have been d. has been
5. He ________ physics for the last two hours. a. has been studied b. was studied c. has studied d. was studying
6. The train hasn't arrived ________. We're still waiting. a. yet b. lately c. already d. ago
7. Mum ________ lunch. I can smell her delicious food. a. had prepared b. has prepared c. is prepared d. would prepare
8. I ________ three emails this morning. I'll send two more. a. send b. had sent c. was sent d. have sent
9. She ________ to the fruitseller's. The apples she has bought are tasty. a. has gone b. had gone c. has been d. had been
10. I ________ squash. I don't even know its rules. a. have never played b. wasn't played c. have ever played d. ever played

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. He has ________ arrived home. Let him relax for a few minutes before you talk to him. a. just b. ever c. never d. yet
2. I have met some old friends ________. a. yet b. lately c. recently d. b & c
3. I haven't heard from her ________ ages. I hope she is better. a. at b. since c. for d. a & c
4. I haven't heard from her ________ last April. I hope she is better. a. at b. since c. for d. a & c
5. I have watched football matches ________ as long as I can remember. a. so b. such c. since d. for
6. I have watched football matches ________ my childhood. a. so b. such c. since d. for
7. We haven't met since we ________ to our new villa. a. moved b. move c. were moved d. has moved
8. ________ when have you waited for her? a. For b. Since c. Ago d. a & c
9. ________ how long have you waited for her? a. For b. Since c. Ago d. a & c
10. How long ________ did you start waiting for her? a. for b. since c. ago d. a & c

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. She has never travelled abroad ________. a. already b. ago c. now d. before now
2. This is the first time I have ________ seen a real kangaroo. a. never b. ever c. just d. always
3. This is the first time for me ________ a real kangaroo. a. see b. have seen c. to see d. to seeing
4. Amira is ________ student I have ever seen. a. a clever b. clever c. cleverer d. the cleverest
5. I have never seen a student ________ Amira. a. as clever as b. cleverer than c. the cleverest d. a & b
6. I have never seen a clever student ________ Amira. a. like b. as c. such d. similar
7. She ________ me at five. a. has just called b. had just called c. last called d. never call
8. The last time she called me ________ five. a. at b. was at c. in d. was in
9. She hasn't called me ________ I returned home. a. when b. after c. before d. since
10. She last called me ________ I returned home. a. when b. while c. for d. since
11. She ________ to work here two months ago. a. has started b. hasn't started c. started d. starts
12. It is a week ________ I last went to the club. a. for b. since c. while d. after

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. She ________ to the market. She will be here soon. a. has been b. has gone c. was going d. had been
2. She ________ any meetings since she came here. a. wasn't attending b. doesn't attend c. won't attend d. hasn't attended
3. In the last two hours, I ________ my lessons. a. had revised b. have revised c. revise d. was revised
4. So far, many schools ________ in rural and urban areas. a. will have been built b. are building c. have been built d. were built
5. Oh! I ________ my mobile. What can I do? a. broke b. had broken c. was broken d. have broken
6. He ________ to the bank to draw some money. He will be back in half an hour. a. has been b. has gone c. had been d. was going
7. Dr Tamer ________ four patients so far this morning. a. had seen b. has seen c. saw d. was seen
8. Nothing like this ________ to her. a. had happened b. has never happened c. has ever happened d. happened
9. I haven't travelled to my hometown by bus ________ my early childhood. a. when b. lately c. for d. since
10. This is the most difficult problem I ________ challenged. a. had ever been b. have ever c. had ever d. have ever been

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
11. Leen ________ in all her assignments and is going to start at university next month. a. has succeeded b. has been succeeding c. succeeds d. will be succeeding
12. Haytham broke his arm and has to stay in bed, so he ________ watching a lot of programs recently! a. is b. has been c. was d. will be
13. Cairo streets are muddy because it ________ all day. a. had rained b. had been raining c. has been raining d. will be raining
14. He is still doing the homework. He ________ it for four hours now. a. has done b. has been doing c. had been doing d. was doing
15. Scientists in China ________ researching the side effects of COVID-19 for years; they are still doing this task. a. had been b. have c. have been d. were
16. A long time has passed ________ I saw him. a. as long as b. because c. since d. until
17. Rania ________ as a doctor for the last ten years. a. has been working b. worked c. had worked d. is working
18. We ________ our old friends for a year. They are too busy. a. hadn't met b. have met c. haven't met d. haven't been met

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
19. Although he ________ to France twice this year, he's going again next week. a. goes b. has gone c. has been d. have gone
20. They ________ each other for a long time. a. have known b. have been knowing c. had been knowing d. are knowing
21. A terrible accident ________ place on the highway from Benha to Mansoura. a. was taken b. has taken c. has been taken d. had taken
22. You needn't make a food delivery. I ________ a good meal already. a. was cooking b. have cooked c. was cooked d. had cooked
23. It's been four months since we ________ our friend Ahmed in Dubai. a. had visited b. visited c. was visited d. visit
24. My friend ________ a health problem since he lived in a town surrounded by three factories. a. has had b. had had c. had d. has been
25. Sorry for being late, Sir. Please, let me in; I ________ stuck in heavy traffic. a. have been b. have gone c. was been d. had been
26. I haven't met the boss ________. It's my first time to meet him. a. yet b. before c. already d. never
27. My uncle ________ as a teacher for 15 years. Now, he is an accountant in a small company. a. worked b. was working c. has worked d. works
28. I haven't seen Leen ________ the last time we met in the café. a. while b. when c. for d. since
29. He wanted to know ________ I have been a member in this sports club. a. since how long b. for how long c. for when d. how long ago
30. My car ________ down, so I have to call a mechanic at once. a. was broken b. has broken c. had broken d. has been breaking

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
31. I ________ the kitchen all morning and mum says it's still dirty. a. have cleaned b. clean c. am cleaning d. have been cleaning
32. What ________ your mother for her birthday tomorrow? a. have you been buying b. have you bought c. you bought d. had you bought
33. ________ I started my job, I have met important people from all over the world. a. For b. Since c. Until d. When
34. The committee members' disagreement ________ caused a serious problem. a. has b. have c. was d. were

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
35. Which of the following sentences is structurally correct? a. I haven't seen him for the last time we met in the club. b. I haven't seen him since the last time we met in the club. c. I haven't seen him since the last time we have met in the club. d. I didn't see him since the last time we met in the club.
36. Which of the following is structurally correct? a. He is exhausted because he hadn't been working all day. b. He was exhausted because he has been working all day. c. He is exhausted because he has been working all day. d. He is exhausted because he had worked all day.
37. Ali last caught fish when he was in Alexandria. This means that a. Ali didn't catch fish since he was in Alexandria. b. Ali didn't catch fish when he was in Alexandria. c. Ali hasn't caught fish since he was in Alexandria. d. Ali has been catching fish since he returned from Alexandria.
38. My friend arrived at school a moment ago. This means that he a. has just arrived b. just has arrived
39. Which of the following is structurally correct? a. Have you already traveled to Luxor by plane? b. Have you ever traveled to Luxor by plane? c. I haven't already traveled to Luxor by plane. d. I haven't traveled to Luxor by plane already.
40. Which of the following is structurally correct? a. Since years, I haven't played handball. b. It's years since I have last played handball. c. It's years since I played handball. d. I had played handball years ago.

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
41. She has been to America. This means that she ________ there. a. is still b. is no longer c. will be d. won't be e. hasn't arrived f. will arrive
42. Mai has never flown before this time. This means a. it is the first time for Mai to fly b. Mai has flown once before this time c. we do not know if Mai has flown before or not d. Mai has flown before that time
43. Rodayna is the cleverest girl I have ever known. This means a. Rodayna is as clever as some girls I have known before b. Rodayna is less clever than some girls I have known before c. Rodayna is cleverer than some girls I have known before d. Rodayna is cleverer than all girls I have known before
44. I have never eaten such a delicious meal. I mean that a. I am not enjoying the meal I am eating b. I am enjoying the meal I am eating c. the meal I am eating is not delicious d. the meal I am eating is not as delicious as meals I have eaten before
45. I have never gone fishing. This means a. this is the first time for me to go fishing. b. I haven't ever gone fishing. c. I went fishing but I can't remember. d. I have ever gone fishing.
46. My wife hasn't gone out since Friday. This means that a. my wife last went out on Friday. b. my wife didn't go out on Friday. c. my wife has gone out on Friday. d. my wife hasn't gone out on Friday.
47. I have lived in Edfu since 1999. This means a. I lived in Edfu in 1999. b. I have been lived in Edfu since 1999 c. I started to live in Edfu in 1999. d. I left Edfu in 1999.

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
1. I ________ Oliver Twist all day. It's very interesting, but I'm only on chapter 2. a. have been reading b. had read c. had been reading d. have read
2. Your clothes are really dirty. What ________? a. had you been doing b. had you done c. have you been doing d. will you be doing
3. My father ________ travelled abroad; he prefers to spend his time sitting calmly in his village. a. has ever b. has never c. has already d. had never
4. I miss Leen so much; I ________ her for ages! a. won't see b. hadn't seen c. haven't been seeing d. haven't seen
5. Since the beginning of the Russian-Ukrainian War, the world economy ________ greatly affected. a. has b. had been c. has been d. had
6. ________ doing your home assignment yet? a. Have you finished b. Had you finished c. Will you finish d. Are you finishing
7. They ________ the road to our street recently. a. had paved b. have been paved c. have been paving d. had been paving
8. He hasn't been able to play tennis ________ he sprained his ankle. a. since b. for c. go d. when
9. You can't meet Rokaya because she ________ the science fair. a. has been to b. has gone to c. has gone d. has been
10. They ________ in Benha since 2005. They are still living there. a. had lived b. have lived c. have been living d. had been living

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
11. My sister ________ at university for three years. She comes home every weekend. a. is b. has been c. is being d. had been
12. Naguib Mahfouz's books ________ into many languages. a. have translated b. had translated c. were translating d. have been translated
13. Dr Barakat is funny. He has always ________ to his clinic by scooter. a. come b. been coming c. came d. comes
14. Dr Barakat is funny. He always ________ to his clinic by scooter. a. come b. been coming c. came d. comes
15. The forest fires ________ for three weeks now. We need a lot of rain to put them out. a. has burnt b. have been burning c. has been burning d. have burnt
16. The forest fires ________ for three weeks. Now, there are no trees. a. has burnt b. have been burning c. has been burning d. have burnt
17. She's ________ three letters this afternoon. a. written b. been writing c. wrote d. writes
18. They're angry. They ________ for us for ages. a. had been waiting b. waited c. have waited d. have been waiting
19. They were angry. They ________ for us for ages. a. had been waiting b. waited c. have waited d. have been waiting
20. He was in hospital because he ________ an accident. a. has had b. has been having c. had had d. had been having

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
21. He's in hospital because he ________ an accident. a. has had b. has been having c. had had d. had been having
22. I've ________ this page four times and I still don't understand it all. a. reading b. not read c. been reading d. read
23. I'm dead tired. I ________ for ten hours now. a. had worked b. 've been working c. 've worked d. had been working
24. I was dead tired. I ________ for ten hours then. a. had worked b. 've been working c. 've worked d. had been working
25. Leen is still working; she ________ doing that task yet. a. hadn't finished b. hasn't finished c. won't finish d. has finished
26. She ________ a complete cleaning of the house. Everything is glowing. a. had done b. had been doing c. has been doing d. has done
27. ________ Hurghada before? - Yes, I went there last summer. a. Are you visiting b. Have you visited c. Have you been visited d. Will you visit
28. You ________ the hall; it looks lovely! a. were decorating b. will decorate c. have decorated d. had decorated
29. I ________ the movie you recommended. I'm still watching it. a. have watched b. had watched c. have been watching d. had been watching
30. He ________ three glasses of tea since he returned home. a. had drunk b. has drunk c. is drinking d. has been drinking

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
31. I ________ reading the book you gave me, so we can go out now. a. had finished b. have finished c. have been finishing d. had been finishing
32. I ________ my friend, Tony, since we were in Liverpool. a. have known b. had known c. are knowing d. have been knowing
33. I ________ 2 kilometers to school today. a. have run b. had run c. have been running d. had been running
34. Please help me; I ________ my passport. What can I do? a. had lost b. will be losing c. has been losing d. have lost
35. How long ________? - I wish it would stop. a. has it rained b. has it been raining c. will it be raining d. was it raining
36. Look! Someone ________ that window. a. have broken b. has broken c. has been breaking d. hasn't broken
37. ________ have you been studying English? a. How long b. How long ago c. How much d. When
38. ________ did you start studying English? a. How long b. How long ago c. How much d. When
39. I ________ letters all day. I wonder when I will finish. a. typed b. have been typing c. have been typed d. have typed
40. It's the first time I ________ the skateboard. a. see b. have been seeing c. saw d. have seen
41. Splendid! Has Sama done all the washing-up ________? a. yet b. ever c. already d. since
42. Great news! You ________ the competition. a. 've won b. have been winning c. haven't won d. are winning

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:
43. "It's more than 40 years since I traveled abroad." This means that a. I haven't ever traveled abroad. b. I have traveled abroad forty years ago. c. I last traveled abroad forty years ago. d. I have traveled abroad for forty years.

Read the following passage, then choose the correct answer from a, b, c, or d:

The computer, as well as the chainsaw is a powerful tool. They are of great help. If the chainsaw is misused, the person operating it might find themselves missing a finger. But if the computer is misused, it may lead to disasters. With it, teachers can enhance the classroom experience in a variety of ways. But while the misuse of the computer will probably not result in lost limbs like in chainsaw, there are other dangers that must be considered when computers are brought into a classroom. To investigate the way, technology is currently being used in our school, I recently spoke with Mrs Abeer, who informed me that computers, which are introduced into her classroom, have helped to increase students' motivation and prepare them for life in a technological society. "These kids are probably going to be using some advanced computer programs when they graduate from high school," she noted. "It's important that we provide a proper introduction about them in the classroom." She also observed that the computers have made it easier to promote interdisciplinary learning, so that students can work on a project or topic that relates to more than one subject faster. With all of these benefits come a few drawbacks. Mrs Abeer also added that some teachers think that using computers in the educational process is useless and others use them in a way that makes students get easily distracted by the many temptations of the internet, and that many students are quite adept at making it look as if they are doing class work when they are really checking their emails or watching videos. While students are surfing educational websites, video games may make students lose their concentration. These diversions are rather benign in comparison to the other things hiding in the corners of the World Wide Web.

1. This passage is mainly about the ________. a. importance of chainsaw b. pros and cons of computers c. pros of chainsaw d. cons of computers
2. People use some ________ like a chainsaw to accomplish their work. a. devices b. systems c. methods d. tools
3. The improper use of the computer can cause ________ results in comparison to the chainsaw. a. disastrous b. greedy c. favourable d. acceptable
4. While surfing the internet for studying, students could be ________ by video games. a. concentrated b. addicted c. distracted d. focused
5. Some students are experts at violating the classroom rules by ________. a. surfing educational websites b. checking their emails c. working on a project d. using advanced programs
6. Although using modern technology may cause some demerits, man will ________. a. try to find other alternatives b. stop using it c. claim it is dangerous d. never do without it
7. According to the passage, the ways that teachers apply to encourage students to use the internet ________. a. vary from one school to another b. are the same at all schools c. hinder students' understanding d. can protect students
8. Unless students were aware of the dark sides in the World Wide Web, they would ________. a. be successful b. achieve their goals c. suffer a lot d. lead a comfortable life

Read the following passage, then choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:

In recent years, minority-owned businesses in the US have faced both unprecedented opportunities and significant risks. Civil rights activists have long argued that minority groups, such as Blacks and Hispanics, struggle to succeed in business due to lack of access to substantial orders from large companies. In response, Congress passed laws requiring federal contractors to make efforts to engage minority subcontractors. Many government agencies have set goals to allocate public works contracts to minority businesses. Corporate response has been positive, with contracts for minority businesses increasing from $77 million in 1972 to $1.1 billion in 1977, and projections for the early 1980s estimating over $53 billion annually. However, this growth brings risks for minority businesses. Many are small, requiring large investments in equipment, plants, and staff to handle subcontracted work. If their contracts are reduced, they may face financial difficulties due to fixed expenses. The corporate world can be frustrating for small businesses, with complex bidding processes that consume time and resources. Additionally, white-owned companies may attempt to exploit the opportunity by forming partnerships with minority firms, sometimes setting up minority businesses as "fronts" instead of true partners. Finally, some minority businesses become overly reliant on a single corporate customer, which limits their ability to expand and make their business sustainable.

20. What is the central idea of the text? a. The challenges faced by large companies in securing contracts. b. The risks and opportunities for minority-owned businesses in securing federal contracts. c. The success of white-owned businesses in gaining government contracts. d. The importance of small businesses in the US economy.
21. Which of the following describes a risk faced by minority businesses as they expand? a. Increased competition from large businesses. b. Difficulty in meeting federal contract goals. c. Financial overextension and fixed expenses from growing too quickly. d. Lack of interest from potential partners such as large companies.
22. Which of the following strategies do large companies use to support minority businesses? a. Making exclusive deals with minority businesses. b. Creating partnerships with minority enterprises. c. Offering discounts on their products. d. Reducing federal contract requirements.

Read the following passage, then choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:

Jennifer was thinking in her room. She wanted a shelter there since false gossip about her began circulating at the academy last week. Not that Jennifer had ever been considered a social butterfly. She preferred to live indirectly through the stories of her more unashamed friends: late night celebrating, associating with girls, childish tricks. Still, she had taken to be more by herself than usual since the accusations surfaced and that thing made her on thin ice. She was up for consideration for the highly popular Blauvelt Award, a scholarship recognizing "academic integrity and promise".

A student had given headmaster Jack an anonymous "tip" that Jennifer had cheated on several tests. The accusations were laughable. Jennifer had long been a clever student at the academy. She lacked a natural intelligence - this was true. However, she compensated for this shortage through carefulness and insistence; she was very thorough in her studies and exhibited an almost relentless determination. Still, the accusations had given the recommendation committee pause.

On Friday, Jennifer had been called to Mr Jack grand office, where she was asked copious questions about her recent exams. The experience was quite shocking. It was an elite school. Most of its students came from honoured backgrounds. This was not the case for Jennifer. Her family had little money. She attended the school on a full scholarship. The Award would help her family pay for college. So, it was with the same diligence which she applied to her studies that Jennifer planned to reveal her accuser.

Read the following passage, then choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:

We should be proud of all our Egyptian celebrities in all times. We had many great men in the 19th and 20th century who did many wonderful patriotic actions. Mustafa Kamel, an Egyptian nationalist leader, was born in 1874. Witnessing Egypt's struggle under British control, Kamel's spirit of patriotism ignited. He, along with other students, formed a secret society advocating for Egypt's independence. Their actions led to their expulsion from Egypt, forcing Kamel to complete his education in France. While abroad, Kamel immersed himself in European political ideas, particularly those emphasizing national identity and self-determination. He actively participated in Egyptian student communities, keeping the fight for his homeland alive. Kamel's fiery speeches and passionate articles resonated deeply with the Egyptian people. He challenged the legitimacy of British rule, highlighting its detrimental impact on Egypt's economy and social fabric. He emphasized the importance of education and self-reliance, urging Egyptians to strive for a future free from foreign domination. Despite facing threats and imprisonment by the British authorities, Mustafa Kamel remained undeterred. He continued to mobilize the masses, organizing peaceful protests and advocating for a boycott of British goods. His unwavering determination and eloquent articulation of Egypt's aspirations earned him the title, "The Voice of the Nation." Mustafa Kamel's hard efforts, though tragically cut short by illness in 1908, significantly contributed to the rise of Egyptian nationalism. He laid the groundwork for future generations who ultimately achieved Egypt's independence in 1952. After Mustafa Kamel's death in 1908, leadership of the Egyptian nationalist movement fell to Mohamed Farid. Farid, a wealthy aristocrat, continued advocating for Egyptian independence from British rule, taking control of the newly formed National Party founded by Kamel. His influence helped keep the fight for Egyptian self-determination alive.

Mustafa Kamel was helpless and isolated from Egypt abroad. d. The British emphasized the importance of education and self-reliance.

Read the following passage, then choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:

Our body has many organs which do their functions properly. Each one has a specific job to do to survive. For example, your heart, a tireless pump working faithfully day and night, is vital to your very existence. But what happens when this vital organ is starved of the oxygen it needs? A heart attack, also known as a myocardial infarction, occurs when blood flow to a part of the heart muscle is severely reduced or cut off completely. This disrupts the heart's ability to pump blood effectively, threatening your health and even your life. The culprit behind most heart attacks is atherosclerosis, a gradual narrowing of the coronary arteries. These arteries supply blood rich in oxygen to the heart muscle. Over time, fatty deposits called plaque buildup on the artery walls, restrict blood flow. A piece of plaque may rupture, triggering the formation of a blood clot that can completely block the artery. This blockage starves the heart muscle of oxygen, leading to cell death and a heart attack. Symptoms of a heart attack can vary from person to person. The classic image of someone clutching their chest in pain is common, but discomfort can also manifest as pain in the arm, shoulder, jaw, back, or even nausea and lightheadedness. Recognizing these diverse symptoms and seeking immediate medical attention is crucial. Early intervention can minimize heart damage and improve your chances of a full recovery. Thankfully, there are steps you can take to reduce your risk of a heart attack. Maintaining a healthy lifestyle is key. This includes eating a balanced diet low in saturated fats, exercising regularly, managing stress, and maintaining a healthy weight. Additionally, controlling conditions like high blood pressure and cholesterol can significantly decrease your risk. By understanding the causes and symptoms of heart attacks, and by prioritizing a heart-healthy lifestyle, you can empower yourself to take charge of your cardiovascular health.

Who do you think is the most/least successful? In order to answer this question, we first need to define success. Everyone has different definitions. Many of us place a great value on performance, achievements, or reaching various goals. Others associate success with material goods, measuring it by how much one can acquire. Others measure success by how much they can contribute to the well-being of their families, or to the community and society in general. There are many other definitions, but mostly success is associated with becoming rich, famous, and respected at work.
Yet, it doesn't have to be that way. Being successful could mean simply being satisfied with oneself and one's career. For many people, a successful person is someone who feels that his or her work and life, in general, offer an exceptionally high degree of satisfaction. But how does one get from here to there? How does one reach this feeling of satisfaction with life and career? It seems that successful people consistently do two things: they use their natural abilities in their work, and they set career and life goals.
In other words, successful people choose careers where they can use their natural abilities or do what comes naturally to them. For example, successful teachers are people who instinctively know how to help people learn, and the best doctors are those who know how to listen to people. Successful people know where they are starting from, and what direction they want to take their lives and careers, even if it is something as simple and wonderful as raising a happy family.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines that are programmed to think and learn like humans. Over the past few decades, AI has seen rapid development and has become an integral part of various industries, such as healthcare, finance, transportation, and entertainment. AI systems are capable of performing tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition, decision-making, and language translation. One of the most well-known applications of AI is in virtual assistants, like Siri and Alexa, which can perform a wide range of tasks based on voice commands. In healthcare, AI is being used to improve diagnostics and treatment plans by analyzing large amounts of medical data. For instance, AI algorithms can help detect diseases like cancer at an early stage by examining medical images. In finance, AI is used for fraud detection, algorithmic trading, and personalized financial advice. However, the rise of AI also raises ethical concerns and challenges. Issues such as data privacy, job displacement, and algorithmic bias need to be addressed to ensure that AI is developed and used responsibly. As AI continues to evolve, it is crucial to strike a balance between leveraging its potential and mitigating its risks.

With the birth of the big cities and their exploding population has become the problem of getting rid of their wastes. Many people believe that these cities will eventually bury themselves in their own rubbish unless something is done, and done quickly. They support their argument by stating that in 1985 Mexico City produced 6,000 more tons of rubbish than could be collected. The worries about land pollution, shortage of natural resources (especially wood and oil), increasing costs of raw materials and growing unemployment all point to one solution: recycling. Recyclable waste falls into seven basic groups: paper, glass, plastics, metals, rags, bones and organic matter. The first five groups can be recycled to produce similar products a second time. Organic matter can be used to feed animals or used as fertilizer. Bones produce fat, gelatin and calcium which can be used in animal feed or to make filters. Among the many advantages of recycling are: saving valuable natural resources, preventing land pollution, saving manufacturers' money, creating a lot of jobs (to recycle the waste of Cairo would need 17,000 - 20,000 collectors, 6,000 sorters plus transporters, processors .etc.), creating new small businesses which can supply many of the major industries and generating money (e.g. in Cairo, if all the rubbish that could be recycled was recycled, it would generate $ 73, 467 a day). For recycling to be a success, it needs the support of the government in supplying areas for dumping, sorting and recycling, and to launch a campaign to persuade the public to co-operate. It needs experts to find suitable people or companies to recycle each material. All the way along the line, from the collector to the seller of the recycled product, there has to be an acceptable income. In this way, meeting this demand is easier in the developing world, where people are on lower incomes, than in developed countries where expectations of reward are higher.

Choose the correct Arabic translation from a, b, c or d:
1. Sleep is nature's best nurse. People, in particular, usually sleep to renew their energy and look joyful. a. النوم هو أفضل ممرض للطبيعة، وعادة ما ينام الناس على وجه الخصوص لتجديد طاقتهم والظهور بمظهر مبهج. b. النوم هو أفضل ممرض طبيعي، وعادة ما ينام الناس على وجه الخصوص لتجديد طاقتهم والظهور بمظهر مبهج. c. النوم هو أفضل ممرضة للطبيعة، وعادة ما ينام الناس على وجه الخصوص لتجديد طاقتهم والظهور بمظهر مبهج. d. النوم هو أفضل ممرض طبيعي، وأحياناً ما ينام الناس لتجديد طاقتهم والظهور بمظهر مبهج على وجه الخصوص.
2. Healthcare is considered an important element in increasing production. A healthy worker can work harder and more efficiently than a sick one. a. تعد الرعاية الصحيحة أحد العناصر الهامة في زيادة الإنتاج، فالعامل السليم يمكنه أن يعمل بجدية أكبر وكفاءة أعلي من العامل المريض. b. تعد الرعاية الصحية أحد العناصر الهامة في زيادة الإنتاج، فالعمل السليم يمكنه أن يعمل بجدية أكبر وكفاءة أعلي من العامل المريض c. تعد الرعاية الصحية أحد العناصر الهامة في زيادة المنتجين، فالعامل السليم يمكنه أن يعمل بجدية أكبر وكفاءة أعلي من العامل المريض. d. تعد الرعاية الصحية العنصر الأهم في زيادة الإنتاج، فالعامل السليم يمكنه أن يعمل بجدية أكبر وكفاءة أعلي من العامل المريض.

Choose the correct English translation from a, b, c or d:
1. لابد من إيجاد حلول عملية لمواجهة مشكلة نقص الغذاء في معظم دول العالم، فعدد السكان في تزايد مستمر وذلك يتطلب زيادة مستمرة في الإنتاج الغذائي. a. We have to find scientific solutions to confront the problem of food shortage in the world. The global population is constantly decreasing and this inquires a continuous increase in food production. b. We have to find practical solution to confront the problem of food shortage in most of the countries of the world. The global population is constantly increasing and this enquires a continuous increase in food products. c. We have to find scientific solutions to confront the problem of food shortage in the world. The global pollution is constantly increasing and this requires a continuous increase in food production. d. We have to find practical solutions to confront the problem of food shortage in most of the countries of the world. The global population is constantly increasing and this requires a continuous increase in food production.
2. لكي لتجنب الأمراض والعدوي، يجب علينا الحفاظ على نمط حياة صحي وذلك من خلال نظام غذائي صحي متوازن، وممارسة الرياضة بانتظام، والحصول على قسط كاف من النوم. a. To avoid diseases and infection, we should maintain a healthy lifestyle by having a balanced diet, exercising regularly and getting little sleep. b. To avoid infectious diseases, we should maintain a healthy lifestyle by having a balanced diet, exercising regularly and getting enough sleep. c. To avoid diseases and infection, we should maintain a healthy lifestyle by having a balanced diet, exercising regularly and getting enough sleep. d. To avoid diseases and infection, we should entertain a healthy lifestyle by having a balanced diet, exercising regularly and getting enough sleep.

Choose the correct Arabic translation from a, b, c or d:
1. It has been proven that meditation helps create a stress-free work environment and improves productivity rates. a. لقد ثبت أن التأمل يساعد على تهيئة بيئة عمل خالية من التوتر ويحسن أسعار الإنتاجية. b. لقد ثبت أن التأمل يساعد على تهيئة بيئة عمل خالية من التوتر ويحسن معدلات الإنتاجية. c. لقد ثبت أن الوساطة تساعد على تهيئة بيئة عمل بلا توتر وتحسين معدلات الإنتاجية d. قد ثبت أن التأمل يساعد في خلق بيئة عمل بلا توتر وتحقيق إنتاجية كبيرة.
2. Do you know that vitamins are essential for a good, healthy and balanced diet as they protect us from many fatal diseases, so they are called protective foods? a. هل تعلم أن الفيتامينات ضرورية للنظام الغذائي الجيد الصحي والمتوازن، لأنهم يقوموا بحمايتنا من كثير من الأمراض المزمنة.لذلك يُطلق عليهم الأطعمة الوقائية ؟ b. هل تعلم أن الفيتامينات ضرورية للوجبة الجيدة الصحية والمتوازنة، لأنهم قاموا بحمايتنا من كثير من الأمراض المزمنة، لذلك يطلق عليهم الأطعمة المحمية؟ c. هل تعرف أن الفيتامينات ضرورية للوجبة الجيدة الصحية والمتوازنة، لأنهم يقوموا بحمايتنا من كثير من الأمراض المستدامة.لذلك يطلق عليهم الأطعمة الواقية؟ d. هل تعرف أن الفيتامينات ضرورية للنظام الغذائي الجيد الصحي والمتوازن، لأنهم قد يقوموا بحمايتهم من كثير من الأمراض المزمنة، لذلك يطلق عليهم أطعمة الحماية؟

Choose the correct English translation from a, b, c or d:
1. لابد أن تتعلم كيفية إدارة الوقت وترتيب أولوياتك حتى تنجز الكثير من المهام المختلفة بكفاءة وفي وقت مناسب، وهذا بالتأكيد يجعلك شخصاً منتجاً وناجحاً في الحياة. a. You must learn how to arrange your time and manage your priorities in order to accomplish a lot of different tasks efficiently promptly. This definitely makes you a productive and successful person in life. b. You must learn how to manage your time and arrange your priorities to accomplish a lot of different tasks sufficiently promptly. This definitely makes you a productive and successful person in career. c. You must learn how to manage your time and arrange your priorities to accomplish a lot of different tasks efficiently in a timely manner. This definitely makes you a productive and successful person in life. d. You must learn when to manage your time and arrange your priorities in order to refresh a lot of different tasks efficiently in a timely manner. This definitely makes you a consumer and successful person in life.
2. للحفاظ على صحتك البدنية ووظيفتك، احرص دائما على التحلي بالصبر والأمل، ولا تدع ضغوط الحياة تدفعك للاستسلام. وتذكر أن الاستراحة بين الحين والآخر ضرورية لتجديد طاقتك ومواصلة العطاء. a. To keep your physical healthy and keep your job, always strive to remain patient and hopeful, and never let life's pressures lead you to give up. Remember that taking occasion breaks is essential to recharge your energy and continue giving your best. b. To maintain your physical health and keep your job, always strive to remain patience and hopeful, and never let life's pressures leads you to give up. Remember that taking occasion breaks is important to charge your energy and continue giving your best. c. To maintain your physical health and keep your job, always strive to remain patient and hopeful, and never let life's pressures lead you to give up. Remember that taking occasional breaks is essential to recharge your energy and continue giving your best. d. To maintain your physical healthy and keep your job, always strive to remain patient and hopeful, and never let life's pressures lead you to give up. Remember that taking occasional breaks is important to charge your energy and continue giving your best.

metadata.lesson_title: EXERCISE 1: General Exercises on Language (LET, ALLOW, MAKE, STOP)

text:
Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:

1. She hasn't had her knives _____ yet. a. sharpen b. to sharpen c. sharpened d. sharpening
2. She got the curtains _____ yesterday. a. cleans b. cleaning c. cleaned d. to clean
3. I'll have my bookcase _____ next week. a. delivered b. deliver c. will deliver d. to deliver
4. We _____ our washing machine installed last night, so now we can wash our clothes. a. get b. got c. allow d. make
5. Stupid managers usually _____ less work done. a. prevent b. allow c. get d. let
6. I went to the oculist's to _____ my eyes checked. a. allow b. let c. prepare d. have
7. The boss got the employees _____ till late at night. a. to work b. be working c. work d. worked
8. I got my hair _____ before the wedding. a. cut b. cuts c. cutting d. to cut
9. My mother has me _____ the shopping every week. a. do b. done c. to do d. had done

metadata.lesson_title: EXERCISE: MINI TEST 6 Clear the Confusion

text:
Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:

1. A dove representing peace in a story is an example of a. Symbolism b. Climax c. Theme d. Flashback 
2. A hint of something that will happen later in the story is called a. Imagery b. Symbolism c. Foreshadowing d. Resolution 
3. A narrator whose credibility is questionable is called a. Reliable Narrator b. Point of View c. Unreliable Narrator d. Theme 
4. A scene set in a time earlier than the main story is a a. Non-linear Narrative b. Flashback c. Resolution d. Pacing 
5. A scene that takes the reader back to an earlier time is called a a. Symbolism b. Climax c. Flashback d. Theme 
6. A story told out of chronological order is a a. Climax b. Imagery c. Non-linear Narrative d. Setting 
7. A struggle between a hero and a natural disaster is an example of a. Imagery b. Climax c. Conflict d. Exposition 
8. A struggle between characters or forces in a story is the a. Theme b. Climax c. Conflict d. Foreshadowing 
9. Descriptive language that appeals to the senses is called a. Dialogue b. Imagery c. Symbolism d. Rising Action 
10. Events that happen after the climax leading to the resolution form the a. Falling Action b. Plot c. Imagery d. Dialogue 
11. If a story jumps back to an earlier time to give background, this is called a a. Flashback b. Rising Action c. Exposition d. Climax 
12. Non-literal language that creates imagery is called a. Theme b. Resolution c. Figurative Language d. Conflict 
13. The angle from which a story is told is the a. Conflict b. Point of View c. Imagery d. Dialogue 
14. The background information that sets up the story is called the a. Climax b. Exposition c. Resolution d. Flashback 
15. The final outcome or solution in a story is called the a. Theme b. Setting c. Resolution d. Point of View 
16. The location and time in which a story takes place is referred to as the a. Theme b. Setting c. Climax d. Imagery 
17. The main idea or message behind a story is the a. Theme b. Plot c. Climax d. Foreshadowing 
18. The main idea or underlying message of a story is the a. Setting b. Plot c. Theme d. Exposition 
19. The moment of highest suspense or emotional intensity is called the a. Climax b. Resolution c. Dialogue d. Conflict 
20. The part of a story that wraps up loose ends is the a. Climax b. Resolution c. Symbolism d. Point of View

Choose the correct answer from a, b, c or d:

21. The part where events start building toward the climax is known as the a. Falling Action b. Conflict c. Rising Action d. Theme 
22. The person, animal, or figure in a story is called a. Setting b. Plot c. Character d. Dialogue 
23. The sequence of events in a narrative is known as the a. Plot b. Theme c. Pacing d. Conflict 
24. The sequence of main events in a story is called a. Climax b. Plot c. Theme d. Dialogue 
25. The speed at which a story progresses is known as its a. Pacing b. Climax c. Setting d. Foreshadowing 
26. The spoken exchanges between characters are called a. Exposition b. Imagery c. Dialogue d. Plot 
27. The turning point or moment of greatest tension is the a. Resolution b. Foreshadowing c. Climax d. Symbolism 
28. The use of an object to represent an idea is known as a. Symbolism b. Imagery c. Dialogue d. Theme 
29. The use of hints about what will happen later is known as a. Plot b. Theme c. Foreshadowing d. Dialogue 
30. The way an author hints at future events is called a. Imagery b. Foreshadowing c. Dialogue d. Theme 
31. The words spoken by characters are called a. Imagery b. Dialogue c. Figurative Language d. Theme 
32. What is the term for language that uses figures of speech for effect? a. Imagery b. Theme c. Figurative Language d. Pacing 
33. What is the term for the perspective of the narrator in the story? a. Conflict b. Theme c. Point of View d. Setting 
34. When an object in a story represents an idea, it is called a. Climax b. Conflict c. Symbolism d. Plot 
35. When two characters speak to each other in a story, the writer is using a. Flashback b. Plot c. Theme d. Dialogue 
36. Which element indicates how fast or slow the events unfold? a. Pacing b. Setting c. Plot d. Climax 
37. Which element of narrative shows a writer's style and emotional tone? a. Point of View b. Emotion and Voice c. Theme d. Resolution 
38. Which element often contains a problem that must be solved in a story? a. Theme b. Setting c. Plot d. Imagery 
39. Which part of a narrative introduces the main characters and setting? a. Climax b. Rising Action c. Exposition d. Falling Action 
40. Which part of a story provides sensory details to create mental images? a. Dialogue b. Theme c. Imagery d. Resolution 
41. Which term refers to the order of events in a narrative? a. Sequence b. Imagery c. Climax d. Rising Action

Is it advisable that we work all the time without having rest or entertainment? The answer is that we can never go on working. From time to time, we should stop to have some rest and some recreation to have fun. Shouldn't we? If we don't, we could make mistakes and our work might get worse. An overworked person may end up losing their ability to think clearly and having no energy to carry on working.
Sleep is one way in which we can renew our energy. Sports and games are also important for brainworkers, who stay in closed offices and don't have a good chance to exercise their muscles. Swimming, walking and other outdoor activities are excellent and they seem very good for all people, so we shouldn't waste our weekends. People make full use of them by being in the open spaces. Having forgotten all about our work and the worries of life, we can then enjoy ourselves to the full and return the next day to work with our energy fully renewed.
Change has a magic effect on man's health and his work. Only clever people are those who can make a balance between their work and their personal lives. They are hardworking when they do their jobs and they live the happiest moments when they aren't working.

Artificial intelligence, which is abbreviated as AI, is the intelligence by the machines under which they can perform different functions with a significant human help. With the help of AI, machines will be able to think, solve problems, plan things, etc. It is the simulation of human intelligence by machines. In the field of technology, artificial intelligence is evolving rapidly day by day and it is believed that in the near future, AI is going to change human life and will end all the crises of the world by sorting out the major problems. Our life in this modern age depends largely on computers. They have become almost impossible to think about life without computers. We need computers in everything that we use in our daily lives so it becomes very important to make computers intelligent to make our lives easier. The main theory of AI is developing computers which imitate human intelligence. AI is now widely used in the fields of healthcare for fast and accurate diagnoses and operating on patients, in the field of industry through using robots to do dirty and hard work but that resulted in a big loss to people's jobs and in the field of education to bring innovative ways of teaching. Because we can't separate ourselves from modern technology which has become an integral part of our lives and changed our lives for good, artificial intelligence is the future of innovative technology.

Throughout human history, many ancient civilizations have come and gone, obtaining and leaving behind valuable knowledge. In the modern days, much research and information in the medical field came from the people of ancient Egypt. Although they lived during the early years of human existence, they have acquired exceptional data. Ancient Egypt has been a major contributor to modern day medicine, for they have given knowledge of hygiene, surgery and the uses of herbs. The Egyptians' need to be clean and hygienic has greatly influenced many people in this time and age, preventing what could cause medical damage. In the modern-day world, there are still some remedies and treatments practiced that was discovered by the Egyptians. Some herbal and natural remedies used in modern day time include mint, garlic and Aloe Vera, while their other herbal medicines provided a base for our everyday medicines, such as antibiotics and pain killers. When it came to pain killers, it is recorded that the ancient Egyptians had an understanding and used natural remedies to control pain long before Europeans figured out the natural powers of such herbs. They understood and used herbs and natural elements to protect themselves from disease and parasites. In Egypt, they treated worms with pomegranates and garlic, later proven by modern medical studies that the ancient Egyptians were absolutely right to treat worms with them. This is because; modern scientists confirmed that the contents in pomegranate and garlic can actually kill worms. As for everyday remedies, the Egyptians also used the powers of the Aloe Vera plant to calm burning skin and mint to relieve severe pain. The ancient Egyptians relied greatly on their natural resources and provided a stepping stone for the medicine in today's world, although we would never know about their discoveries if it hadn't been for the way they kept a written record of them on walls of temples and pieces of papyri.

move - moved → يتحرك - يُحرك. Example: I couldn't move the heavy box alone.
move out → يرحل من منزل / يخلي مسكن. Example: He said goodbye and moved out.

move into → ينتقل لمنزل أو مقر جديد + (مفعول). Example: The new flat is ready. We will move into it soon.
move away → يبتعد / يعزل من مكان (بدون مفعول). Example: Watch out! Move away. The dog is dangerous.
move away from → يبتعد عن + (مفعول). Example: Watch out! Move away from the dog. It is dangerous.
move on → ينتقل لعمل / نشاط / موضوع / وضع جديد - يواصل (بدون مفعول). Example: I had to finish the tasks in hand before I move on.
move on to → ينتقل للقيام بـ. Example: I had to finish the tasks in hand before I move on to the new one.

raise - raised - raised → فعل متعدي يأخذ مفعول. يرفع: Mr Hussein raised his arm. He wants to make a comment. يجمع (مال): This charity raises money for homeless children. يربي (يرعى): Parents work hard to raise their children.
arise - arose - arisen → فعل لازم لا يأخذ مفعول. ينشأ - يحدث: arise = happen, occur, take place. Example: Problems arise every now and then.
arouse - aroused - aroused → فعل متعدي يأخذ مفعول. يثير - يسبب (مشاعر): arouse = cause a feeling, interest, reaction. Example: The way she talks arouses laughter.
spring - sprang - sprung → jump يقفز. Example: Ahmed sprang out of bed and ran to the kitchen.
spring into action → start acting quickly يتصرف بسرعة. Example: As the man was badly injured, the doctors sprang into action.
full of → filled with مليء بـ. Example: This young man is full of energy.
filled with → مليء بـ. Example: This bottle is filled with tap water.
fulfilled → feeling satisfied or rewarded شاعر بالرضا أو القناعة. Example: After finishing the task, Sama was tired but fulfilled.

metadata.lesson_title: My Day in the Hospital / Emergency Call

text: Every morning, Nurse Sarah kicks off her day with a strong cup of coffee before her 12-hour shift begins. She is always preparing herself mentally for the challenges ahead. As soon as she arrives, she checks patients' charts and reviews updates from the night shift. The hospital is buzzing with activity - doctors rushing, monitors beeping, and patients waiting. Sarah's first task is administering medication to her patients. Some have been waiting anxiously, while others are resting quietly. She has cared for many patients over the years, but each day brings new surprises. One elderly patient, Mr. Ramy, has been recovering from surgery and needs extra attention. Sarah makes sure he is comfortable before moving on. By midday, emergencies arise - a car accident victim is rushed in, and the team springs into action. Sarah assists the doctors, ensuring everything runs smoothly. Despite the chaos, she remains calm and focuses on her duties. "Teamwork makes the dream work," she often says, relying on her colleagues for support. By evening, Sarah has checked on all her patients and has documented their progress. She feels exhausted but fulfilled. As she heads home, she reflects on the day; some moments were heartbreaking, but many were rewarding. Nursing is a never-ending rollercoaster, but Sarah wouldn't trade it for anything.

Operator: 123, what's your emergency?
Caller: My neighbor has collapsed! He was walking downstairs when he suddenly grabbed his chest and fell down. I have never seen him like this before!
Operator: Have you checked his pulse?
Caller: His pulse has been checked, but he hasn't been breathing, so CPR has been started. His wife said he complained about chest pain earlier, and his medication hadn't been taken.
Operator: Hold on... Help is being sent. Has he regained consciousness???
Caller: No! By the time the ambulance has arrived, I'll try again.
Operator: Don't worry. The ambulance is on the way already and the paramedics will take over the situation.

Doctors and nurses work hard every single day to care for patients. Their job is never easy. They spend long hours in hospitals, and they often miss meals or sleep. Many doctors visit patients, write reports, and make quick decisions that can save lives. Nurses give medicine, check charts, and comfort patients. They know their work is important, but it is also very tiring. Hospitals have become really busy recently. Over the years, doctors have faced many challenges, and they have learned how to stay strong. Right now, many doctors are seeing patients in crowded clinics, while others are performing operations. Nurses are walking from one room to another, answering questions and giving support. They are always on their feet, and they rarely rest during their shifts. Sometimes they feel exhausted, but they continue because patients depend on them. In fact, they have been working long shifts recently, and they have treated hundreds of patients this week alone. Doctors and nurses also suffer emotionally. They see people in pain, and they sometimes lose patients even after trying their best. This makes them sad, but they still smile and encourage families. They know their sacrifices bring hope and healing. For years, they have been showing true dedication and compassion, and their efforts have inspired many young people to choose medicine as a career.

The Present Perfect Simple is used in both active and passive voice. Active voice formation: I/You/We/They + have + past participle. He/She/It + has + past participle. Examples: I have watched a film. Omar has bought some CDs. Passive voice formation: Object + have/has + been + past participle. Examples: A film has been watched by me. Some CDs have been bought by Omar. Uses of the Present Perfect Simple: 1. To express actions or situations that started in the past and have not ended yet. I've studied English since 2013. I still study English. She has worked in this school for years. 2. To express situations that happened in the past without mentioning when they occurred. This includes: a. Expressing previous experiences: I've never played squash. b. Expressing what the subject has accomplished so far: My daughter has learned to speak English. She can speak English now. c. Expected or anticipated events: The train hasn't arrived at the station yet. 3. To express past actions or situations that have an effect on the present (the effect sentence is in present tense). I've eaten a lot of food, so I feel very full. 4. To express actions or situations that can be repeated using extended time expressions such as today, tonight, this morning, this week, this month, in the last year. I have drunk two cups of coffee today. I can drink more cups of coffee today. 5. To express the future after time conjunctions (the present simple can be used for the same purpose). After my father has left home, I'll ask mum to give me more money.

Important notes on have/has been usage: have/has been to means went and returned. have/has gone to means went and hasn't returned yet. have/has been in + for + period of time means went and stayed in for a period. Important notes on adverbs with Present Perfect: just (تواً), already (بالفعل), recently (مؤخراً), lately (مؤخراً), yet (حتى الآن). I have done the shopping already equals I have already done the shopping. The manager has just left the office. Ahmed hasn't done his homework yet. Note that yet comes in negative and interrogative sentences. We have visited some friends lately or recently. Time expressions with for and since: for is followed by the duration of the action: a while, a moment, half an hour, three minutes, ten years, two seconds, five days, a decade, six months, ages (مدة طويلة), the last..., a long time, long, two centuries, a short time, as long as I can remember. Examples: I've had this mobile for 10 years. He's been here for 6 months. since is followed by a time expression indicating when the action started: six o'clock, Monday, March, summer, yesterday, last month, his arrival, his departure, her death or birth, then (منذ ذلك الحين), 26th September, 2013. Examples: I've lived in this flat since 2015. He's been here since May. Additional grammar notes: If there is a clause after since, its tense is past simple and sometimes present perfect. I haven't eaten anything since I left home this morning. You can use verb-ing after since if the subject of the phrase after it is the same as the subject before it. She has been very busy since having her second baby. Remember that since is sometimes used to mean because. He is tired since he has been working all day.

Question forms with Present Perfect: Since when + have/has + subject + past participle? Example: Since when have you lived in Aswan, Omar? (Omar still lives in Aswan). For how long + have/has + subject + past participle? How long + have/has + subject + past participle + for? Example: For how long have you lived in Aswan, Omar? (Omar still lives in Aswan). How long ago + did + subject + infinitive? Example: How long ago did you live in Aswan, Omar? (Omar no longer lives in Aswan). The difference between the Present Perfect and the Past Simple: Present Perfect focuses on the result of the event. They have lost their money means they are now broke. It describes an event that happened very recently. He has just arrived. It describes an event that may change. I haven't met Mohammed Salah means Mohammed Salah is still alive and you may meet him. It describes an event completed in a period that has not ended yet. It hasn't rained this morning means the morning hasn't ended yet and it may rain. It describes an ongoing event or habit. He has always been fit means he continues to be fit and hasn't changed. It describes an event that occurred at an unspecified time in the past. He has traveled to America. Past Simple focuses on the event itself, a past event with no relation to the present. They lost their money. It describes an event that happened at a specific time in the past. He arrived two hours ago. It describes an event that cannot change. I didn't meet President Sadat means President Sadat has passed away. It describes an event completed in a period that has ended. It didn't rain this morning means the morning has ended and it didn't rain. It describes a discontinued event or habit. He was always fit means he was fit before but is now unfit. It describes an event that occurred at a specified time in the past. He traveled to America yesterday.

Special expressions with Present Perfect - First Experience: Subject + have/has + never + past participle + before now. Example: Ahmed has never visited Paris before now. This is the first time + subject + have/has + ever + past participle. Example: This is the first time Ahmed has ever visited Paris. This is the first time + for + subject/object pronoun + to + infinitive. Example: This is the first time for Ahmed to visit Paris. A different experience - Notice the use of the following patterns to distinguish a situation or experience from previous ones: Subject + have/has + never + past participle + noun + like + noun. Example: I have never seen a clever student like this one. We have never eaten delicious food like that. Subject + have/has + never + past participle + a + noun + as + adjective + as + noun. Example: I have never seen a student as clever as this one. We have never eaten food as delicious as that. Subject + have/has + never + past participle + a/an + comparative adjective + noun + than. Example: I have never seen a student cleverer than this one. We have never eaten food more delicious than that dish.

Special expressions with Present Perfect - Last Experience - Notice the use of the following patterns to express the last occurrence of a situation or experience: Subject + haven't/hasn't + past participle + since/for. Example: Zamzam hasn't gone out since Monday. Subject + last + past tense + in/on/at + past time expression. Example: Zamzam last went shopping on Monday. The last time + subject + past tense + was in/on/at + past time expression. Example: The last time Zamzam went shopping was on Monday. Subject + haven't/hasn't + past participle + since + past simple clause. Example: Ahmed hasn't met his grandmother since he was in Assuit. Subject + last + past tense + when + past simple clause. Example: Ahmed last met his grandmother when he was in Assuit. Starting point or duration - Notice the use of the following patterns to express when an event started or how long it took: Subject + have/has + past participle + for + period. Example: Mohammed has lived in Aswan for 21 years. Subject + have/has + past participle + since + a point in time. Example: Mohammed has lived in Aswan since 2005. Subject + started/began + to infinitive or gerund + period + ago. Example: Mohammed started to live in Aswan 21 years ago or in 2004. It is/has been + period + since + past simple clause. Example: It is 21 years since Mohammed started to live in Aswan.

The Present Perfect Continuous - Formation: I/You/We/They + have + been + verb-ing. Example: I have been waiting for half an hour. He/She/It + has + been + verb-ing. Example: Rokaya has been studying English for two years. Uses of the Present Perfect Continuous: An action that started in the past and extended to the present moment, often with since or for. Note that the Present Perfect can be used for the same purpose. Examples: Ayman and Ashraf have been talking for the last two hours. Radwa hasn't been working for that company for long. Has Mr Mohammed been teaching at this school for a long time? What have the children been doing for the last few minutes? If there is no since or for, the Present Perfect Continuous is more general to mean recently or lately. Examples: Recently, he has been feeling unwell. Sara has been watching too much television lately. Hassan has been feeling a little worried about his job. Lisa has not been doing exercise.

The difference between the Present Perfect Simple and Continuous: The Present Perfect Simple is used to indicate an action that is completed and has an effect or result in the present. The effect or result came from the completion of the action. Examples: He's had his meal, so he is full. Rokaya has done the housework, so she can watch TV now. The Present Perfect Continuous is used to indicate an action that has an effect or result in the present. The effect or result came from the action itself, not its completion. Examples: I've been having my meal so there are plates, spoons and cups all over the table. Rokaya has been doing the housework, so she looks very tired. The Present Perfect Continuous is used to focus on the effect of continuity or time duration on the action. Example: Wake up, Nada. You have been sleeping for 10 hours. The Present Perfect Simple only, not the Present Perfect Continuous, is used when mentioning number or quantity. Words indicating this include: two/three/many/a few/few/several/a lot of/lots of/all/plenty of plus plural object. Examples: He has drunk three cups of tea this evening. He has drunk at least a litre of tea this evening. I have met many customers recently. The Present Perfect Simple only, not the Present Perfect Continuous, is used with instantaneous verbs or stative verbs (verbs of perception, feelings, senses, possession, etc.). Examples: A fast car has crashed into a rock by the road. We have owned this flat for 15 years. She has known these friends for a year.
